mnemonics.title: PLC Stages Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: R-D-D-U-R: Requirements → Development → Deployment → Use → Retirement

mnemonics.title: Sustainable Design Principles
mnemonics.content: S.E.E.D: Select efficient algorithms, Eliminate waste, Enable lazy loading, Defer processing

acronyms.usage: Framework for managing environmental impact from conception to disposal
Data center energy efficiency metric (Total/IT energy)
Discarded electronic devices requiring special disposal
Total GHG emissions in CO2 equivalent
Total energy used throughout software lifecycle

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a bar chart where the 'Use' phase is the tallest bar (60-90%), followed by 'Manufacturing' (10-20%), with 'Development' and 'Disposal' as small bars. This shows where sustainability efforts should focus.

Picture an iceberg: The visible tip is 'Direct Energy' (electricity for computation), while below water is 'Indirect Energy' (manufacturing, cooling, infrastructure) - much larger but often overlooked.
Think of a calculator: Energy (kWh) × Carbon Intensity Factor (kg CO2/kWh) = Carbon Footprint. Lower either factor to reduce impact.

analogies.analogy: Like a car's fuel consumption during driving far outweighs the energy used to manufacture it, software's energy during operation dominates its lifecycle impact.
Writing efficient code is like insulating a house - the upfront effort saves energy (and money) continuously over time.
Virtualization is like carpooling - instead of one person per car (one app per server), multiple passengers share the same vehicle, reducing total resources needed.
Right-sizing infrastructure is like buying clothes that fit - too big (over-provisioned) wastes material, too small (under-provisioned) doesn't work.
